items.title: Map nano-banana-2 to nano-banana-pro for upstream in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update implements a mapping from `nano-banana-2` to `nano-banana-pro` in the image generation and editing handlers. The change ensures compatibility with the upstream nanobanana service, which only accepts specific model names. Key files modified include the handlers for image generations and edits, along with necessary environment configurations.

items.title: Exciting Multi-Provider Support Added to Python SDK in S D K 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a `provider` parameter for the Python SDK's image, video, and audio resources, aligning with the TypeScript SDK. New providers such as `producer`, `kling`, and others have been added to enhance functionality. Both TypeScript and Python READMEs have been updated with examples to guide users in utilizing the new multi-provider capabilities.

items.title: Enhance Document Sidebar Icon Alignment in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the vertical alignment of icons and text in the document sidebar by using inline-flex and center alignment. Additionally, long document names now truncate with an ellipsis for better readability. Icon sizing and alignment have also been fixed to ensure a polished user experience.

items.title: Enhance CLS Dashboard Management Skills in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new management skill for CLS Dashboards, allowing users to list, create, and delete dashboards via a direct API interface. The implementation includes a script and documentation to facilitate usage, ensuring compatibility with the latest API requirements. Key functionalities have been tested and verified for seamless operation.

items.title: Update NanoBanana Port Configuration in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: The NANOBANANA_BASE_URL port has been updated from 3000 to 8000 to align with the K8s Service configuration. This change ensures that the `platform-service-openaihk-nanobanana` service correctly exposes the intended port. Testing will confirm that the `nano-banana` model functions properly via the specified endpoints.

items.title: Fix X402 null values crashing Kong serialization in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses an issue where the X402 `/auth` response was sending null values for `application_id` and `credential_id`, causing a crash during query serialization in Kong. The fix involves omitting these fields entirely from the response and adding a helper function to handle potential null values defensively. As a result, X402 requests will now return a successful response instead of a server error after payment verification.

items.title: Streamline GLM Models by Removing Broken Ones in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update removes three broken GLM models (glm-5, glm-4.5, glm-4-flash) from the frontend, ensuring a cleaner and more efficient user experience. The GLM group now features only the functional models: glm-5.1 and glm-4.7. Additionally, necessary changes were made to related constants, types, and internationalization files to reflect this update.

items.title: Removed 7 Broken GLM Models in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes seven broken GLM models from the system, including glm-5-turbo and glm-4-plus, ensuring cleaner functionality. The OpenAPI specifications, service cost rules, and tests have been updated accordingly. Five operational models remain, enhancing the reliability of the platform.

items.title: Exciting New NanoBanana Models for Image APIs in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for the new `nano-banana`, `nano-banana-2`, and `nano-banana-pro` models in the image generation and editing APIs. The OpenAPI specifications have been updated to include these models, and six new cost test cases have been added to ensure accurate pricing. All cost tests are passing locally, paving the way for enhanced functionality in image processing.

items.title: Enhance Announcement Display with Markdown in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces the use of the `VueMarkdown` component to render announcement content as markdown, allowing for clickable links and rich text formatting. Links will open in new tabs, enhancing user experience while maintaining the display of plain text announcements. Key files modified include the AnnouncementPopover component and the API models.

items.title: Reverting taskId extraction changes in Platform Service 🔄
items.summary: This update reverts the previous fix that extracted taskId from the upstream response. The change aimed to prevent null crashes but has been rolled back to restore the original behavior. The modification includes adjustments in the core.ts file, impacting how taskId is handled in the response.

items.title: Enhance SDK with Multi-Provider Support in S D K 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces multi-provider support for Images, Video, and Audio resources, allowing users to specify providers dynamically. Additionally, it adds several missing task endpoints and transitions the build process from `tsc` to `tsup` for improved module resolution and output formats. These changes enhance flexibility and usability for developers working with the AceDataCloud SDK.

items.title: Exciting New NanoBanana Model Support for Image Endpoints in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for the NanoBanana models, allowing them to be routed through the OpenAI-compatible image endpoints. Key changes include the addition of model configurations, format transformations between OpenAI and NanoBanana, and updates to the model listing. This enhancement ensures seamless integration and improved functionality for image generation and editing tasks.

items.title: Enhance Payload Normalization for Accurate Billing in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new function to normalize payload string values, ensuring reliable JsonLogic evaluations for billing. Numeric strings are coerced to integers or floats, and non-numeric strings are converted to lowercase to prevent case mismatches. These changes address production billing anomalies and enhance the accuracy of cost rule evaluations.

items.title: Enhance Document Sidebar with Icons in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces document icons in the sidebar tree by adding an `icon_url` to the `IDocument` TypeScript interface. Icons are conditionally rendered for all document types, enhancing visual identification. The implementation ensures that documents without icons still render correctly, maintaining a clean user experience.

items.title: Enable Auth-Free Access for OpenAI Models in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the OpenAI models metadata endpoint to the list of APIs that can be accessed without authentication. The API ID `df57157e-2a4f-43f6-870b-fd3c530827e6` has been added to `SKIP_AUTH_API_IDS`, allowing users to retrieve model information without billing requirements. Other endpoints will continue to enforce authentication as usual.

items.title: Streamline API by Removing Deprecated Fields in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the deprecated `price`, `estimation`, and `unlimited` fields from the `Api` model, enhancing code clarity and maintainability. The `price` field has been replaced by `cost`, while `estimation` was never utilized, and `unlimited` was redundant. A Django migration is included to drop these columns effectively.

items.title: Streamline API Model by Removing Deprecated Types in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the deprecated `IApiPrice` and `IApiEstimation` types from the API model, along with their associated fields. The changes include a Django migration to drop these columns, ensuring a cleaner and more efficient codebase. The removal of these fields will help streamline API responses and improve maintainability.

items.title: Clean Up Deprecated Api Fields in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the deprecated `price`, `estimation`, and `unlimited` fields from the `Api` model, streamlining the codebase. The `price` field has been replaced by `cost`, while `estimation` was never utilized, and `unlimited` was redundant. A Django migration is included to drop these columns, ensuring a cleaner API response.

items.title: Streamline Api Model by Removing Deprecated Fields in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the deprecated `price`, `estimation`, and `unlimited` fields from the `Api` model, which were either unused or replaced by newer implementations. A Django migration has been included to drop these columns from the database. The changes enhance the clarity and efficiency of the API model.

items.title: Enhance Document Model with Icon URL in Backend 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces an `icon_url` field to the Document model, allowing for the storage of document icon URLs. This enhancement will enable the display of icons for parent documents in the PlatformFrontend, improving user experience. The necessary migration has been generated and applied.

items.title: Enhance Free API Cost Configuration in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new cost configuration for the free `/openai/models` API endpoint, setting its consumption to zero. A corresponding database record has been created to support this change. The implementation has been tested to ensure that it integrates smoothly with existing systems.

items.title: New OpenAI-Compatible Models Endpoint Added in Platform Service 🤖
items.summary: We've introduced a new `/v1/models` endpoint that returns a list of available models in the OpenAI SDK format. This update includes the `ModelsHandler` and a configuration for over 80 supported models, enhancing compatibility with tools like Banana Mall. The changes ensure seamless integration for users accessing model listings.

items.title: Enhance GLM Support and Fix Routing in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for 12 GLM models across multiple providers, ensuring robust failover capabilities. Additionally, the routing for GLM models has been fixed in `check.py`, preventing potential errors during health checks. The changes enhance the reliability and performance of the checker system.

items.title: Refactor X402 Settlement Context Storage in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the storage of X402 settlement context by saving it in `ApiUsage.metadata` during the `/auth` phase, streamlining the process. The `/record` endpoint now retrieves this context using `trace_id`, eliminating the need for query parameters. Additionally, the `x402_settle` forwarding from the Lua plugin has been removed, simplifying the architecture without requiring changes to the PlatformService workers.

items.title: Enhance Kling Videos OpenAPI Spec for Clarity in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the Kling Videos OpenAPI specification by correcting model descriptions, durations, and response examples. Key changes include clarifying the capabilities of each model and ensuring accurate documentation of audio support. These adjustments enhance the overall accuracy and usability of the API documentation.

items.title: Enhance X402 Payment Settlement Process in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update moves the X402 payment settlement from the `/auth` phase to the `/record` phase, ensuring users are only charged when the API call is successful. Additionally, the default timeout for X402 has been increased from 600 to 3600 seconds to better accommodate long-running API calls. The changes also include passing a base64-encoded settlement context through the request chain for improved processing.

items.title: Revamped create-service skill for enhanced clarity in Index 🚀
items.summary: The create-service skill has undergone a comprehensive rewrite, expanding from 18 to 24 steps and from 8 to 10 phases. Key additions include coverage for packages, translations, MCP server structure, Dify plugin details, and end-to-end billing verification. This update also introduces a service types table and a new non-chat pricing strategy, enhancing user guidance and service integration accuracy.

items.title: Enhance MCP Server with OAuth 2.0 Support in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces OAuth 2.0 as a new authentication option for MCP servers, complementing existing methods. Users can now authorize servers via a new 'Authorize' button, which triggers a popup for the OAuth flow. Additionally, the update includes new API methods and types to facilitate OAuth interactions, enhancing the overall user experience.

items.title: Enhance OAuth 2.1 Support for MCP Integration in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `MongoOAuthClientProvider`, which implements the MCP SDK's `OAuthClientProvider` interface, enabling MongoDB persistence and secure token storage. Key features include new handler actions for OAuth authorization flow and updates to the `MCPClientManager` for improved connectivity with OAuth-type MCP servers. The implementation supports the full OAuth 2.1 flow, enhancing security and user experience.

items.title: Enhance Documentation Experience with Collapsible Tree and Method Badges in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update collapses the documentation sidebar tree by default, improving the user experience by preventing all nodes from being expanded initially. Additionally, HTTP method badges (POST, GET, PUT, DELETE, PATCH) have been added to API document titles, enhancing clarity with color coding. The `IDocument` interface has also been updated to include an `api_method` field, which will require a corresponding backend update.

items.title: Enhance DocumentIndexSerializer with API Method Field in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an `api_method` field to the `DocumentIndexSerializer`, allowing the extraction of HTTP methods from `Api.definition.paths` for each document. This enhancement enables the frontend to display method badges in the document sidebar tree, improving user experience. Additionally, the implementation ensures that non-API documents return a null value for the `api_method` field.

items.title: Sync Local Files with New Skills and Updated Docs in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the new `create-service` skill and adds several MCP test scripts to enhance functionality. Additionally, the project documentation in `CLAUDE.MD` has been updated to reflect the latest changes, and the workflow for updating profiles has been modified for improved efficiency. These changes aim to streamline development and testing processes.

items.title: Update Veo Model Documentation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update clarifies the image count rules for various Veo models in the integration documentation. Key changes include correcting the image requirements for `veo2-fast` and `veo31-fast-ingredients`, as well as updating the model list to reflect all 7 models. The introduction pages have also been revised to accurately represent the new model count.

items.title: Fix model override for ingredients2video action in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update enforces the use of the 'veo31_fast' model for the ingredients2video action, replacing the previous 'veo31_fast_ingredients' model. This change addresses compatibility issues with the upstream mountsea service, which only accepts 'veo31_fast' for this action. The implementation ensures that other models remain unaffected for different actions.

items.title: Standardize Veo Model Name for Clarity in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update standardizes the model name in the veo-video SKILL.md to `veo31-fast-ingredients`. The change enhances clarity and consistency in the documentation, ensuring users have the correct information for utilizing the ingredient mode effectively.

items.title: Standardize Veo Model Name in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update standardizes the Veo CLI output, tests, and README to consistently use the model name `veo31-fast-ingredients`. Key files modified include the README, test commands, and output handling, ensuring clarity and accuracy in model references. These changes enhance the usability and reliability of the CLI for users.

items.title: Standardize Veo Model Name to veo31-fast-ingredients in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update standardizes the Veo model name across various components, specifically changing references to 'veo31-fast-ingredient' to 'veo31-fast-ingredients'. Key files modified include types, info tools, and video tools, ensuring consistency in model naming. This change enhances clarity and usability for developers working with the Veo models.

items.title: Standardize VEO Model Name in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update standardizes the ModelSelector dropdown value and label from `veo31-fast-ingredient` to `veo31-fast-ingredients`. This change enhances consistency and clarity in the user interface, ensuring users see the correct model name.

items.title: Standardize VEO Model Name in Docs 📚
items.summary: This update standardizes the model name from `veo31-fast-ingredient` to `veo31-fast-ingredients` in the OpenAPI specification. This change ensures consistency in documentation and improves clarity for users. The modification involved a minor update in the JSON file.

items.title: Improve X402 Settlement Process in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update defers the X402 on-chain settlement to a background asyncio task, preventing Kong's timeout issues. By returning a 200 response immediately after verification, the system enhances user experience while maintaining security through EIP-712 signatures. This change ensures smoother payment processing without blocking the auth response.

items.title: UI Bug Fixes for Aichat2 in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses several UI bugs in the Aichat2 component. Key changes include adding guards to dialog components to prevent unwanted rendering, adjusting z-index for the wallet button, and implementing silent error handling for CORS issues. These improvements enhance user experience by ensuring cleaner dialog interactions and better error messaging.

items.title: Revert veo model name to veo31-fast-ingredients in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update reverts the model name back to `veo31-fast-ingredients` across various files, correcting an earlier mistake made in PR #322. Key changes include updates to cost rules, OpenAPI specs, and documentation in both English and Chinese. The fix ensures consistency and accuracy in the API and documentation.

items.title: Align Model Name with OpenAPI Spec in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the model name for `veo31-fast-ingredient` in the cost rules to match the OpenAPI specification. Both the API billing rule and service display pricing files have been modified to ensure consistency with the external interface contract. This alignment enhances the reliability of the cost rules and prepares for future updates in the PlatformService worker.

items.title: Enhance DB Resilience for Payment Processing in x402 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the handling of database unavailability in the verify and settle views of the X402 payment system. It ensures that valid payments can still be processed even when the database is unreachable, preventing disruptions caused by DNS resolution failures. Key changes include catching all database exceptions and allowing on-chain settlements to proceed without prior verification records.

items.title: Enhance Error Handling and Logging in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves error handling by extracting upstream 4xx error messages in the `createTask()` function, providing clients with more accurate feedback instead of generic messages. Additionally, all `console.log` statements have been converted to structured JSON logging for better parsing and analysis in CLS, enhancing overall log clarity and utility.

items.title: Fix URL issues and enhance request handling in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a trailing slash issue in the FACILITATOR_URL that was causing double-slash URLs, leading to 404 errors and JSON parsing failures. Additionally, it fixes the request body handling in the x402 authentication flow, ensuring that pre-read bytes are correctly passed to the handler. Debug logging has also been added for better visibility into facilitator responses.

items.title: Enhance error handling for task creation in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves error handling during task creation by extracting specific HTTP 4xx error messages from upstream responses. Instead of retrying and throwing a generic error, the system now throws a `BadRequestError` or `ForbiddenError` immediately, providing clearer feedback to clients. This change applies to both `mountsea` and `cqtai` workers, ensuring better error reporting and user experience.

items.title: Fix billing bug for Kling videos in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical billing issue where Kling video generations were incorrectly billed at 0.0 credits. The root cause was identified in the payload merge order, which has now been corrected to prioritize request fields over response fields. This change ensures accurate billing for Kling videos while maintaining the integrity of LLM chat billing.

items.title: Fix null crash by extracting taskId in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a TypeError caused by the `_createTask()` method returning a null response from the upstream API. A null check has been added to ensure that if `data.taskId` is missing, a clear `ApiError` is thrown instead of passing a null object downstream. This change enhances error handling and prevents crashes in the application.

items.title: Fix API Call Failures for Aichat2 in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves the Agent 405 error by implementing a new `AgentOperator` class that correctly sets the base URL and headers for API calls. Additionally, it fixes the token source in `ConnectorManager.vue` to ensure that the correct Bearer token is used for authentication, preventing connector API call failures. These changes enhance the reliability of the chat and connector functionalities.

items.title: Enhance Deploy Script to Surface Failures in x402 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the deploy script to remove the '|| true' clause, ensuring that deploy failures are now surfaced instead of being ignored. Additionally, it triggers a redeploy for the APPEND_SLASH fix, improving overall deployment reliability.

items.title: Automatic i18n Updates from zh-CN in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces automatic translation updates for internationalization (i18n) based on changes from the zh-CN source. Key modifications include extensive updates to language files for Arabic, German, English, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Russian, and Traditional Chinese, enhancing the multilingual support of the platform.

items.title: Fresh deploy triggered in x402 🚀
items.summary: An empty commit has been made to initiate a fresh deployment run. This action addresses previous failures caused by TKE CLB connectivity timeouts from GitHub Actions runners.

items.title: Enhance Kling Model Support in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for the new `kling-v2-6`, `kling-v3`, and `kling-v3-omni` models in worker validation. It also allows flexible durations between 3-15 seconds for V3/V3-Omni models and updates the pricing table to reflect new billing structures. Additionally, the OpenAPI specification has been revised to include all current models and parameters.

items.title: Admin Publisher Bug Fixes and Enhancements in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses several critical bugs in the admin publisher interface. The task state display now correctly shows the state instead of the status, while the channel column has been improved to display icons and names. Additionally, user details such as avatar and email are now visible in the task table, and a permission guard has been added to restrict access for non-superusers.

items.title: Fix chat toolbar bugs for smoother experience in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses several issues with the chat toolbar, including hiding the connector button when no OAuth providers are available, which eliminates the 'No connectors available' error message. The toolbar layout has been improved using flexbox to prevent UI overlap with the wallet button, ensuring all buttons are clickable. Additional margin adjustments have been made to enhance usability across different viewports.

items.title: Exciting New Kling V3 Features Added in Backend 🚀
items.summary: We've introduced five new promotional blurbs for Kling V3, highlighting its flexible 3-15 second duration, native audio generation, and per-second billing. The updates also include support for V3-Omni video reference input and V2.6 pro audio capabilities. These enhancements aim to elevate user experience and streamline AI video creation.

items.title: Exciting New Model Support and Features in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for the kling-v3, kling-v3-omni, and kling-v2-6 models in the ModelSelector, enhancing user options. A new GenerateAudioSelector toggle has been added, allowing users to generate audio with specific models. Additionally, the DurationSelector is now reactive, adapting to the selected model's capabilities, and the default model has been updated to kling-v2-5-turbo.

items.title: Enhance Kling Video Models in Skills 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces support for the kling-v3, kling-v3-omni, and kling-v2-6 models in the models table. A new `generate_audio` parameter has been added for synchronized audio generation, and the duration for v3 models has been updated to allow flexibility between 3 to 15 seconds. Additionally, examples have been updated to recommend using kling-v3 as the preferred model.

items.title: Exciting New Model Support for Kling in Dify 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for the `kling-v2-6`, `kling-v3`, and `kling-v3-omni` models in the plugin YAML. Additionally, the duration description for V3 has been updated to allow a flexible range of 3-15 seconds. A new `generate_audio` boolean parameter has also been added across the YAML, Python tool, and client, enhancing functionality.

items.title: Enhance Kling OpenAPI Spec with New Models in Docs 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces `kling-v2-6`, `kling-v3`, and `kling-v3-omni` to the `/kling/videos` model enum. The duration field has been updated to support a flexible range of 3-15 seconds, replacing the previous fixed options. Additionally, a new `generate_audio` boolean parameter has been added, and the default model description is now set to `kling-v2-master`.

items.title: Exciting new Kling model support added in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for the `kling-v2-6`, `kling-v3`, and `kling-v3-omni` models within the `KlingModel` Literal type. Additionally, the `Duration` type has been updated to allow for flexible durations between 3-15 seconds for V3 models. The `generate_audio` parameter has also been added to relevant video generation tools, enhancing functionality across the board.

items.title: Exciting New Model Support for Kling in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for Kling V3, V3-Omni, and V2.6 models, enhancing billing rules for various modes and audio generation options. The OpenAPI specification has been updated to include these new models, along with comprehensive documentation in Chinese. Additionally, 23 new cost tests have been added to ensure robust functionality across all model combinations.

items.title: Exciting New GLM Model Group Added to Chat Interface in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new GLM model group featuring five models: glm-5.1, glm-5, glm-4.7, glm-4.5, and glm-4-flash. Additionally, internationalization entries for both English and Chinese have been added to enhance user experience. The changes aim to expand the capabilities of the chat interface with advanced AI models.

items.title: Exciting New GLM Models Added to ephone Checker Config in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces 12 new GLM model capabilities to the ephone checker configuration. The models include glm-5.x, glm-4.x, and glm-3-turbo, each with specified ranks to enhance performance. Key testing will ensure that the checker correctly integrates these models and that API calls route effectively to the ephone.

items.title: Exciting New GLM Model Support Added in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for 12 GLM models via the ephone provider, featuring a markup ratio of 31.537. Key changes include the addition of cost rules, an API cost file, and updates to the OpenAPI specification. All cost tests for the new models have passed successfully, ensuring robust integration.

items.title: Automatic i18n Updates from zh-CN in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces automated translation updates for internationalization (i18n) based on changes from the zh-CN source. Key modifications include updates to multiple language files, enhancing the localization experience across various languages. The changes aim to improve user accessibility and ensure consistency in translations.

items.title: Enhance Admin Dashboard with Task Detail Drawer in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a click-to-view detail drawer for publish tasks in the admin publisher dashboard. Users can now access comprehensive task details, including state, channel, document, user, URL, failure reasons, timing info, and metadata. The implementation includes internationalization support for both English and Chinese.

items.title: Fix APPEND_SLASH to Ensure Supported Endpoint Works in x402 🚀
items.summary: This update disables the APPEND_SLASH setting in Django's configuration to prevent unnecessary redirects that were causing empty responses for the /supported endpoint. By setting APPEND_SLASH to False, we ensure that clients behind the Tencent stgw proxy receive the correct JSON response, enhancing client discovery functionality. The change has been tested to confirm that other endpoints remain functional.

items.title: Enhance dependencies with pywxdump in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the pywxdump>=3.0 dependency to the project, ensuring enhanced functionality. The poetry.lock file has been regenerated to reflect the new dependency tree, optimizing package management. These changes will improve the overall performance and maintainability of the project.

items.title: Automated i18n Updates from Chinese Translations in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This PR introduces automatic updates for internationalization (i18n) translations based on changes from the Chinese (zh-CN) source. Key modifications include updates to multiple language files, enhancing the application's multilingual support. The changes improve user experience by ensuring accurate translations across various languages.

items.title: New Admin Publisher Dashboard Unveiled in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: We've introduced a comprehensive admin Publisher dashboard at `/console/admin/publisher`. This feature includes statistics overview cards, a channel distribution table, and a task list with filters and pagination. Additionally, we've added internationalization support for both English and Chinese, enhancing accessibility for all users.

items.title: Enhance Publish Task Stats and Filters in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new API endpoint, `PublishTaskStatisticsView`, which provides aggregate statistics for publish tasks, including total counts and breakdowns by state and channel. Additionally, the filters for both `PublishTask` and `PublishConfig` lists have been extended to include state, channel_id, and validity fields, improving data retrieval capabilities for the admin dashboard.

items.title: Fix x402 record handling and payment format issues in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses critical issues with the x402 payment flow. The `application_id` guard was adjusted to ensure the x402 check executes properly, preventing silent drops of usage records. Additionally, the payment payload structure has been corrected to align with facilitator expectations, and payment requirements have been unified to use `maxAmountRequired` across all networks.

items.title: Enhance Claude Model Enum with New Addition in Docs 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the 'claude-opus-4-7' model to the Claude chat/completions model enum in the openapi/claude.json file. This addition expands the available options for developers, enhancing the flexibility of the API. The change is straightforward, with a single line added to the enum list.

items.title: Enhance OpenAI Configs with Claude Opus 4-7 in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the claude-opus-4-7 model to the upstream configurations for ephone, openaihk, wcnb, and xiaojingai. Each configuration now includes this model with the same rank as claude-opus-4-6, ensuring consistent routing capabilities across platforms.

items.title: Introducing claude-opus-4-7 model with new pricing in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The claude-opus-4-7 model has been added with the same pricing and configuration as claude-opus-4-6. Key updates include modifications to billing rules, visibility in chat models, and updates to the OpenAPI specifications. All API cost tests have successfully passed, ensuring a smooth integration.

items.title: Enhance API Usage Brief with Elapsed Time in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds an `elapsed` field to the API usage brief endpoint, providing the request duration in seconds. The `ApiUsageBriefSerializer` has been modified to include this new field, ensuring that each usage record now reflects the time taken for the request. This enhancement improves the detail available for API usage analysis.

items.title: Enhance Mountsea Balance Monitoring in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `get_balance_mountsea()` function to monitor credit balances at `dk.mountsea.ai`. The new endpoint retrieves the current credits and calculates the remaining balance in RMB, integrating seamlessly with existing logging systems. This enhancement improves our ability to track and manage credit usage effectively.

items.title: Fix model mapping and action for Veo client in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the mountsea worker's Veo client with Mountsea's official API by correcting model mappings and actions. Key changes include updating the `MODEL_MAPPING`, switching the action for `veo31-fast-ingredients`, clamping the aspect ratio, and removing unsupported resolution parameters. The public API remains unchanged, ensuring compatibility with existing user requests.

items.title: Enhance lyric synthesis for missing aligned_lyrics in Platform Service 🎤
items.summary: This update introduces a new synthesizer that generates `aligned_lyrics` from `aligned_words` when the upstream response omits it. This ensures that downstream consumers, like karaoke displays and the Nexior song player, receive the necessary line-level structure. The implementation has been verified to produce accurate results, maintaining compatibility with existing channels.
